sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 엑손모빌은 2026년 1분기 42억 달러의 순이익을 기록했으며, 일회성 요인을 제외한 조정 순이익은 88억 달러를 달성했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 1분기 순이익은 42억 달러를 기록했으며, 일회성 항목과 시기적 효과를 제외한 조정 순이익은 88억 달러로 집계되었습니다.
주주 환원 정책을 지속하여 분기 중 총 92억 달러를 배당 및 자사주 매입 형태로 주주에게 환원했습니다.

businessChanges.text: 가이아나 지역에서 기록적인 생산량을 달성했으며, 골든 패스(Golden Pass) 1호기에서 첫 LNG 생산을 시작하여 미국 LNG 수출량을 5% 증가시켰습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 영업활동 현금흐름은 87억 달러를 기록했으며, 파생상품 관련 마진 변동을 제외할 경우 138억 달러 수준입니다.
주주 환원액 92억 달러 중 배당금은 43억 달러, 자사주 매입은 49억 달러로 구성되었습니다.

watchItems.text: 이번 분기 실적에 반영된 39억 달러 규모의 비우호적 시기적 효과가 향후 분기에 어떻게 해소되는지 확인이 필요합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 livef8k1q26991.htm NEWS RELEASE Document EXHIBIT 99.1 1Q 2026 Earnings Release FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE May 1, 2026 ExxonMobil Announces First-Quarter 2026 Results • Delivered first-quarter earnings of $4.2 billion , or $8.8 billion excluding unfavorable estimated timing effects of $3.9 billion and an identified item of $0.7 billion • Generated earnings per share of $1.00, or $1.16 excluding the identified item, and $2.09 excluding the identified item and estimated timing effects • Sustained industry-leading reliability and delivered record production in Guyana 1 • Achieved first LNG at Golden Pass Train 1, increasing U.S. LNG exports by 5% • Generated a one-year total shareholder return of 48% and shareholder distributions of $9.2 bill  Normalized numeric aliases: $4.2 billion = 42억 달러; $8.8 billion = 88억 달러; $3.9 billion = 39억 달러; $0.7 billion = 7억 달러; $9.2 billion = 92억 달러; $4.2 = 4.2달러; $8.8 = 8.8달러; $3.9 = 3.9달러; $0.7 = 0.7달러; $1.00 = 1.00달러; $1.16 = 1.16달러; $2.09 = 2.09달러.

2.09 per share, excluding identified items and unfavorable estimated timing effects that unwind in subsequent periods. 3 Cash flow from operating activities was $8.7 billion, or $13.8 billion excluding margin postings, which primarily fluctuate with the fair value of underlying derivatives. 4 Shareholder distributions of $9.2 billion included $4.3 billion of dividends and $4.9 billion of share repurchases, consistent with the company's previously announced plans. "This quarter demonstrated that ExxonMobil is a fundamentally stronger company than it was just a few years ago, built to perform through disruption and across market cycles. Events in the Middle East tested that strength with the safety of our people remaining our top priority.
